dialogueChunks.intro: The wagons rolled in at sunset. The children came running before the wheels stopped turning. I've never heard so much crying and laughing at the same time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The baker's mother made it. Arrow wound and all, she drove that wagon through the gates with a grin on her face. {target_npc} stitched her up while she insisted on unloading the grain first. {quest_ally} says there's enough to last through the winter if we're careful.
dialogueChunks.outro: Those volunteers walked into near-certain death for sacks of grain. And every child eating tonight is proof they were right. Thank you, {player_name}. You made their courage count.

playerResponses.text: The real heroes are the ones who volunteered. I just helped.
Make sure the baker's mother gets the first hot meal.

dialogueChunks.intro: The river crested at midnight and the dam held. The mason climbed out of the water at dawn, shivering so hard he couldn't stand. He just pointed downstream and asked if the houses were still there.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They are. Every single one. {target_npc} carried him to the nearest hearth and wrapped him in every blanket they could find. The families he saved brought food, dry clothes, and quiet gratitude. His wife hasn't let go of his hand since he came home.
dialogueChunks.outro: A man stood in a freezing river all night because he couldn't bear to see his neighbors lose their homes. And because you brought what he needed, {player_name}, he lived to see the sunrise.

playerResponses.text: That man is what community looks like. Take care of him.
Fix the dam properly before the next storm.
Glad it worked out. He's tougher than he looks.

dialogueChunks.intro: All sixty made it. Every last one. The column arrived at {location} ragged, exhausted, and alive. The elder... the elder didn't make it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They found her afterward. She'd held the gates for nearly a full day before the {enemy_type} realized it was one old woman. {quest_ally} says she went down fighting. {target_npc} is building a marker for her at the new settlement's entrance: first thing visitors will see when they arrive.
dialogueChunks.outro: She gave her people a future by giving up her own. And you made sure that future had a clear road to walk on. Her grandchildren are safe, {player_name}. That's what she wanted.

playerResponses.text: She stood taller than any soldier I've met. Honor her well.
I wish we could have saved her too.
